# Resources

## Software

Add installation and setup instructions for the tools used in the course.

Suggested options:

- Quarto for rendering the book and reproducible reports.
- R and RStudio for R-based analysis.
- Python and Jupyter for Python-based analysis.
- Git and GitHub for version control and publishing.

## Course Conventions

Document conventions here, such as:

- File naming.
- Data folder structure.
- Whether examples use R, Python, or both.
- Required package versions.
- Citation style.
- Assignment submission format.

## Dataset Documentation Template

```markdown
# Dataset Name

## Source

## Unit of Observation

## Variables

| Variable | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| participant | categorical | Participant identifier |
| item | categorical | Item identifier |
| condition | categorical | Experimental condition |
| response | continuous | Outcome measurement |

## Exclusions

## Notes
```

## Example File Structure

```text
project-name/
  data/
    raw/
    processed/
  scripts/
  figures/
  report.qmd
```

## Further Reading Placeholder

Add books, articles, tutorials, and package documentation here as the course develops.
